text: Risk management is an important process in any organization. It begins with **identifying risks**, such as financial losses, safety hazards, or project delays. After these risks are found, teams develop **mitigation strategies** to reduce their impact or likelihood. For example, a company might install better security systems to prevent data theft. However, not all problems can be avoided, so **contingency plans** are also necessary. These are backup actions, like having extra suppliers in case one fails. Finally, regular **monitoring** is crucial. Risks can change over time, so teams must review and update their plans. Good risk management helps businesses stay prepared and confident.
